library.code:
--[[ Helper function to create a series from arrays
new_series: a series previously created with ScatterXY.new(name) prefix: prefix of the timeseries, before the index of the array suffix_X: suffix to complete the name of the series containing the X value. If [nil], use the index of the array. suffix_Y: suffix to complete the name of the series containing the Y value timestamp: usually the tracker_time variable
Example:
Assuming we have multiple series in the form:
/trajectory/node.{X}/position/x /trajectory/node.{X}/position/y
where {N} is the index of the array (integer). We can create a reactive series from the array with:
new_series = ScatterXY.new("my_trajectory") CreateSeriesFromArray( new_series, "/trajectory/node", "position/x", "position/y", tracker_time ); --]]
function CreateSeriesFromArray( new_series, prefix, suffix_X, suffix_Y, timestamp )
--- clear previous values new_series:clear()
--- Append points to new_series index = 0 while(true) do
x = index; -- if not nil, get the X coordinate from a series if suffix_X ~= nil then series_x = TimeseriesView.find( string.format( "%s.%d/%s", prefix, index, suffix_X) ) if series_x == nil then break end x = series_x:atTime(timestamp) end
series_y = TimeseriesView.find( string.format( "%s.%d/%s", prefix, index, suffix_Y) ) if series_y == nil then break end y = series_y:atTime(timestamp)
new_series:push_back(x,y) index = index+1 end end
--[[ Similar to the built-in function GetSeriesNames(), but select only the names with a give prefix. --]]
function GetSeriesNamesByPrefix(prefix) -- GetSeriesNames(9 is a built-in function all_names = GetSeriesNames() filtered_names = {} for i, name in ipairs(all_names) do -- check the prefix if name:find(prefix, 1, #prefix) then table.insert(filtered_names, name); end end return filtered_names end
--[[ Modify an existing series, applying offsets to all their X and Y values
series: an existing timeseries, obtained with TimeseriesView.find(name) delta_x: offset to apply to each x value delta_y: offset to apply to each y value
--]]
function ApplyOffsetInPlace(series, delta_x, delta_y) -- use C++ indeces, not Lua indeces for index=0, series:size()-1 do x,y = series:at(index) series:set(index, x + delta_x, y + delta_y) end end

snippet.name: subject_to_angle_smoothing
snippet.function:
if v1 == 0 then return 0 end
if value < 8.5 then return 1 elseif value < 11 then return 2 elseif value <= 13.8 then return 3 elseif value <= 18 then return 4 end
return 0

snippet.name: base_ceiling_bracket
snippet.function:
if v1 == 0 then return 0 end
if value < 20 then return 1 elseif value < 40 then return 2 elseif value <= 120 then return 3 end
return 0

snippet.name: max torque lj adj
snippet.global:
min=0 max=250 max_from_speed=96
k1=200 k2=30 k3=1 k4=1 k5=10
function sign(number) return number > 0 and 1 or (number == 0 and 0 or -1) end
snippet.function: return 250 - value * 20

snippet.name: Angle Staturation
snippet.function:
if value > .3 then return 1 end
return 0

snippet.name: ang_cmd rate
snippet.global: firstX = 0 firstY = 0 is_first = true secondX = 0 secondY = 0 is_second = false
snippet.function:
-- Wait for initial values if (is_first) then is_first = false is_second = true firstX = time firstY = value end
if (is_second) then is_second = false secondX = time secondY = value end
-- Central derivative: dy/dx ~= f(x+delta_x)-f(x-delta_x)/(2*delta_x) dx = time - firstX dy = value - firstY -- Increment firstX = secondX firstY = secondY secondX = time secondY = value
return dy/dx

snippet.name: IMU_LatAccelVal_Ms^3
snippet.function: return value * -9.8

snippet.name: IMU_LatAccelVal_Ms^2
snippet.function: if (v1 == 0 and v2 == 1) then return value * -9.8 end return 0

snippet.name: IMU_LatAccelVal_ms^2_roll_compensated
abs(ang_cmd)
snippet.function: return math.abs(value)

snippet.name: zero
snippet.global:
min=0 max=250 max_from_speed=96
rate_lim = 500
la_deadzone = 0.38
k1=200 k2=20 k3=1.0 k4=1 k5=10
old = 0
function sign(number) return number > 0 and 1 or (number == 0 and 0 or -1) end
function apply_rate_limit(old, new, limit) return math.min(math.max(new, old - limit), old + limit) end
function apply_deadzone(val, deadzone) if math.abs(val) <= deadzone then return 0.0 elseif val < 0.0 then return val + deadzone else return val - deadzone end end
snippet.function: return 0

snippet.name: engaged curvature vehicle model
snippet.function:
curvature = value pressed = v1 enabled = v2
if (pressed == 1 or enabled == 0) then last_bad_time = time end
if (time > last_bad_time + engage_delay) then return value else return 0 end

snippet.name: engaged curvature plan
snippet.function:
curvature = value pressed = v1 enabled = v2
if (pressed == 1 or enabled == 0) then last_bad_time = time end
if (time > last_bad_time + engage_delay) then return value else return 0 end

snippet.name: desired lateral jark
snippet.global: firstX = 0 firstY = 0 is_first = true secondX = 0 secondY = 0 is_second = false
snippet.function:
-- Wait for initial values if (is_first) then is_first = false is_second = true firstX = time firstY = value end
if (is_second) then is_second = false secondX = time secondY = value end
-- Central derivative: dy/dx ~= f(x+delta_x)-f(x-delta_x)/(2*delta_x) dx = time - firstX dy = value - firstY -- Increment firstX = secondX firstY = secondY secondX = time secondY = value
return dy/dx

snippet.name: carState.vEgo kmh
snippet.function: return value * 3.6

snippet.name: engaged_accel_plan
snippet.function:
accel = value brake = v1 gas = v2 enabled = v3
if (brake ~= 0 or gas ~= 0 or enabled == 0) then last_bad_time = time end
if (time > last_bad_time + engage_delay) then return value else return 0 end

snippet.name: Angle Error
snippet.function:
angle_error = last_angle_requested - v1
last_angle_requested = value return angle_error

snippet.name: IMU_LatAccelVal_ms^2
snippet.function: return value * -9.8

snippet.name: desired lat accel
snippet.function: return value * v1^2

snippet.name: engaged_accel_actuator
snippet.function:
accel = value brake = v1 gas = v2 enabled = v3
if (brake ~= 0 or gas ~= 0 or enabled == 0) then last_bad_time = time end
if (time > last_bad_time + engage_delay) then return value else return 0 end

snippet.name: engaged_accel_actual
snippet.function:
accel = value brake = v1 gas = v2 enabled = v3
if (brake ~= 0 or gas ~= 0 or enabled == 0) then last_bad_time = time end
if (time > last_bad_time + engage_delay) then return value else return 0 end

snippet.name: roll compensated lateral acceleration
snippet.function: if (v3 == 0 and v4 == 1) then return (value * v1 ^ 2) - (v2 * 9.81) end return 0

snippet.name: Zero
snippet.function: return (0)

snippet.name: abs(des la accel)
snippet.function: return math.abs(value)

snippet.name: Desired lateral accel (roll compensated)
snippet.function: return (value * v1 ^ 2) - (v2 * 9.81)

snippet.name: Actual lateral accel (roll compensated)
snippet.function: return (value * v1 ^ 2) - (v2 * 9.81)

snippet.name: max torque(calc)
snippet.global:
min=0 max=250 max_from_speed=96
rate_lim = 500
la_deadzone = 0.38
k1=200 k2=20 k3=1.0 k4=1 k5=10
old = 0
function sign(number) return number > 0 and 1 or (number == 0 and 0 or -1) end
function apply_rate_limit(old, new, limit) return math.min(math.max(new, old - limit), old + limit) end
function apply_deadzone(val, deadzone) if math.abs(val) <= deadzone then return 0.0 elseif val < 0.0 then return val + deadzone else return val - deadzone end end
snippet.function:
la = apply_deadzone(v2, la_deadzone) lj = v3
if la == 0.0 then lj = 0.0 end
fla = math.min(math.abs(k1 * la)^k3, max) flj = math.min(math.abs(k2 * lj)^k4, max)
out = fla
flv = math.min(max_from_speed, k5 * v4)
out = out + flv
out = math.max(math.min(out, max), min)
if sign(la) == sign(lj) then out = out - flj else out = out + flj end
if v5 == 1.0 then out = 0.0 end
out = math.max(math.min(out, max), min) out = apply_rate_limit(old, out, rate_lim) old = out
return out

snippet.name: carState.vEgo mph
snippet.function: return value * 2.23694

snippet.name: engaged curvature yaw
snippet.function:
curvature = value / v3 pressed = v1 enabled = v2
if (pressed == 1 or enabled == 0) then last_bad_time = time end
if (time > last_bad_time + engage_delay) then return curvature else return 0 end
